name: Ukwa (African Breadfruit Porridge)
intro: The star of this flavourful and nutritious Igbo dish is ukwa, or African breadfruit. If using dried ukwa, be sure to soak it overnight before cooking, otherwise you can skip the soaking step. The porridge is wonderfully savoury with a hint of spice and sweetness. Ukwa is delicious when cooked plain with a pinch of salt but the additional ingredients included here take it to the next level.

ingredients.item: 1/2 cup bitter leaves (washed, drained and chopped)
vegetable stock cube Salt to taste

method: Wash/rinse the pre-soaked ukwa several times in a colander to remove any grit or dirt.

Place the ukwa, chopped onions, 3 cups of water and baking soda in a large pot. Bring to a boil then cook covered for 45 minutes over medium heat until the ukwa is tender. Check halfway through the cooking time and add extra water if needed.
Once the ukwa is tender, add the palm oil, scotch bonnet chilli, bitter leaves, maize/corn, mushrooms, vegetable stock and salt to taste. Stir and cook for 10 minutes.
